title: SD-JWT VC Type Metadata Document
description: Schema for an SD-JWT VC Type Metadata document per IETF draft-ietf-oauth-sd-jwt-vc-13. §6.2 says: 'A Type Metadata document MAY contain additional top level or subordinate properties. Consumers MUST ignore properties that are not understood.' Receivers must tolerate extensions — but as a sender-conformance test we still flag them so the caller can surface typos / misunderstandings as warnings. additionalProperties: false here lets a separate CheckForUnexpectedParameters condition collect those violations at WARNING severity.

vct.description: REQUIRED per §6.2. The verifiable credential type described by this Type Metadata document.

name.description: OPTIONAL per §6.2. A human-readable name for the type.

description.description: OPTIONAL per §6.2. A human-readable description for the type.

extends.description: OPTIONAL per §6.2. URI of another type that this type extends; processed per §6.4.

extends#integrity.description: OPTIONAL per §7. W3C SRI integrity-metadata string for the extends URI.

display.description: OPTIONAL per §6.2. Type-level display objects per §8.

name.description: REQUIRED per §8. Human-readable name for end users.

description.description: OPTIONAL per §8. Human-readable description for end users.

rendering.description: OPTIONAL per §8. Rendering metadata per §8.1; structure intentionally not constrained here.

claims.description: OPTIONAL per §6.2. Per-claim metadata objects defined in §9.

path.description: REQUIRED per §9.1. Non-empty array of strings, nulls, or non-negative integers selecting one or more claims.

display.description: OPTIONAL per §9. Per-claim display objects defined in §9.2.

mandatory.description: OPTIONAL per §9.3. If true, the Issuer MUST include this claim in the issued credential.

sd.description: OPTIONAL per §9.4. Selective-disclosure constraint: always (MUST be SD), allowed (MAY be SD), never (MUST NOT be SD). Defaults to allowed.

svg_id.description: OPTIONAL per §9. SVG template reference id; alphanumeric+underscore, MUST NOT start with a digit.
